title: Innodata set to report earnings as AI data demand surges
text: Innodata Inc. is preparing to release first-quarter earnings results after market close on Thursday. The AI data services provider is under investor scrutiny regarding its ability to maintain explosive growth momentum, with revenue climbing nearly 48% in the previous year. Analysts anticipate earnings of $0.23 per share on revenue of $72.1 million, reflecting year-over-year growth of 24%, though revenue is expected to remain relatively flat sequentially compared to the fourth quarter's $72.4 million. Wall Street analysts unanimously maintain a strong buy rating, with four analysts establishing an average price target of $91.25 representing 96% upside potential from the current $46.51 share price. However, the stock currently trades significantly below its 52-week high of $93.85, reflecting hesitation about sustained momentum. Investors are focused on several key metrics. Management previously forecast approximately 35% or greater revenue growth for 2026, citing extraordinary momentum across frontier model training, agentic AI, and physical AI applications. Confirmation or revision of this guidance will be closely monitored. Customer concentration risk remains important, particularly regarding the company's largest customer spending plans. The organization is expanding its service portfolio to include higher-value offerings such as model evaluation tools, agent testing, and robotics datasets, positioning itself beyond basic data labeling. North American cloud service providers have increased 2026 capital expenditure guidance to approximately $830 billion a 79% year-over-year increase underscoring robust AI infrastructure demand.
